name: SaaSHub
description: B2B SaaS discovery and software-comparison directory organized around alternatives and competitors; audience is businesses and buyers researching software, making it a long-term, SEO-driven listing rather than a one-day launch.

auth.notes: Free email/password account to submit and claim/manage your service. Listings are reviewed and then live long-term; you can later claim the page to keep details updated.

best_practices: SaaSHub is an evergreen, alternatives-driven directory, so complete every profile field; thorough listings rank and convert over months, not just launch day.
List accurate competitors/alternatives, since SaaSHub's traffic comes from 'X alternatives' searches and being on the right comparison pages drives qualified buyers.
Choose precise categories and keyword-rich tags so your service appears in the relevant B2B comparison and discovery feeds.
Write a description that reads for business buyers and naturally includes the keywords people search to find tools like yours.
Keep pricing clear and accurate; B2B researchers filter and compare on pricing, so vague pricing loses clicks.
Add a clean logo and screenshots and claim the listing so you can respond to reviews and keep information current.
Cross-link your strongest features and integrations; SaaSHub surfaces these on comparison views against alternatives.

questions.label: Tagline / short description
questions.help: One clear line describing the service for business buyers.

questions.label: Description
questions.help: Keyword-rich, buyer-focused explanation of what it does and who it's for.

questions.label: Categories
questions.help: Pick accurate categories so you appear in the right comparison feeds.

questions.label: Alternatives / competitors
questions.help: Name the competing tools your service is an alternative to; this places you on the right comparison pages.
